description:
# Why

bl-32e5 closed the enable/disable/list bit of plugin management but
deliberately stopped at the `participant` block. SPEC ┬¦11's
per-event policy (`required`, `best-effort`, `gating`) is still
hand-edited JSON inside `.balls/config.json` (or
`.balls/plugins/<name>.json` under a master_url hub), with the
same validation-only-at-load ergonomics gap bl-32e5 fixed for the
top-level enable bit. The participant block is also the surface
where the `drop` observe-only rule (validate_drop_policies) lives
ŌĆö exactly the kind of constraint that should fail at command time,
not at the next `bl prime`.

This isn't only a master_url concern. Even standalone repos pay the
JSON-by-hand cost for what is conceptually a per-event toggle. And
the block has an underappreciated semantic ŌĆö `None` vs empty
`subscriptions: {}` differs (legacy fallthrough vs explicit
override-to-nothing). A CLI surface forces us to be honest about
that distinction.

# Concrete deliverable (subject to design discussion below)

Sketch ŌĆö one of:

(a) **Dedicated verb.**
- `bl plugin policy <name> <event>=<kind> [<event>=<kind> ...]`
  ŌĆö upsert per-event policies. `kind` Ōłł {required, best-effort, gating}.
- `bl plugin policy <name> --rm <event> [--rm <event> ...]`
  ŌĆö drop individual subscriptions.
- `bl plugin policy <name> --clear`
  ŌĆö drop the whole participant block (falls back to legacy
  sync_on_change mapping).
- `bl plugin policy <name> --no-legacy`
  ŌĆö write an explicit empty subscriptions {} (suppresses legacy
  fallback, the `None` vs `{}` distinction made visible).

(b) **Fold into `bl plugin enable`.**
- Repeatable `--policy <event>=<kind>` flag on `enable` (and
  maybe `update`). Loses the ability to edit policy without
  re-asserting enable, but keeps the surface smaller.

`bl plugin list`/`bl plugin show <name>` should grow visibility
into the resolved policy per event ŌĆö bl-32e5's text output already
shows `participant=N-events` but doesn't name them.

# Validation

- Reuse `validate_drop_policies` at the command boundary (mirrors
  bl-32e5's `Config::validate` re-run on mutation).
- Reject unknown `<event>` tokens with a list of the legal set
  from `participant::Event`.
- Reject unknown `<kind>` with a list of the three legal values.

# Open design questions (discuss-first)

1. **(a) vs (b).** Dedicated verb composes orthogonally with
   enable/disable/list and lets you flip policy without touching
   the enable bit; the `enable --policy` shape is one fewer
   verb. Per bl-32e5's `enable` semantics, re-running enable
   overwrites `sync_on_change` and `config_file` silently ŌĆö if
   we fold policy in here we extend that footgun. Lean (a).
2. **`None` vs empty `subscriptions: {}`.** Today the loader
   treats `None` as 'fall through to legacy `sync_on_change`'
   and an explicit empty map as 'no subscriptions'. `--clear` and
   `--no-legacy` make the distinction explicit; without them
   users can only express one of the two by hand-editing. Worth
   surfacing or worth collapsing to one knob?
3. **Per-clone overrides.** `LocalPluginEntry` (per-clone
   `.balls/local/config.json`) carries its own participant block
   for per-clone policy override. Should `bl plugin policy` edit
   the committed layer only, or grow a `--local` flag for the
   per-clone variant? Mirrors the `bl remaster` per-clone /
   `--commit` precedent.
4. **List output.** Per-event policy in the default text view, or
   only in `--json` / a new `bl plugin show <name>`? bl-32e5's
   one-line-per-plugin shape can't comfortably carry per-event
   detail.
5. **Removing the `participant` block when the last
   subscription is dropped.** `--rm <last-event>` could leave
   `subscriptions: {}` or implicitly clear back to `None`.
   Different observable semantics (see Q2).
6. **Aliases for the legacy bit.** `bl plugin enable --sync-on-change`
   sets the legacy field. After this ball, that field is
   subsumed by the participant block. Do we deprecate the legacy
   flag, treat it as sugar for a specific subscription set, or
   keep both?

# Out of scope

- Per-task / per-invocation overrides (`--skip NAME`,
  `--required NAME` already exist on every lifecycle command via
  `ParticipantFlags` ŌĆö bl-32e5 punted task-scoped overrides and
  this ball doesn't reopen them).
- GUI / web surfacing.

# Origin

bl-32e5 close-out (2026-05-20). Concerns flagged at delivery:
'Per-plugin `participant` blocks are read-only via this CLI.
`enable` deliberately preserves an existing block but offers no
way to set or clear one. Maintainers still hand-edit JSON for SPEC
┬¦11 policy.' Composes orthogonally with bl-82a4's symlink
reorganization.
title: bl plugin policy: control SPEC ┬¦11 per-event participant knobs
